name: Dehesas de Azuel Loop
name_original: Venta del Charco y Azuel circular desde Cardeña — Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro
description: A challenging 24.0-mile touring cycling route through the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, featuring diverse terrain and scenic vie
region_intro: Touring cycling in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Morena mountain range, characterized by rolling hills and ancient granite boulder outcrops. The terrain includes expansive dehesa pastureland, Mediterranean scrub, and extensive oak woodlands, providing varied inclines and scenic backdrops for cyclists. The deeply incised River Yeguas forms a natural boundary, and the park features unique Pyrenean oak woodlands. These natural features create a varied environment for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro The most popular touring cycling route is Dehesas de Azuel Loop, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses varied landscapes, including dehesa pastureland and granite formations. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cardeña and Azuel Loop, a moderate 16.8 miles (27.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views through oak woodlands and rolling hills. Local touring cyclists also love the Ana Luna Recreation Area – Cardeña loop from Azuel, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through Mediterranean scrub and near riverine areas, often completed in about 2 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro is defined by rolling hills, granite outcrops, and extensive oak woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro, offering a variety of experiences. These include 14 moderate routes and 7 more challenging difficult routes, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The terrain in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro is characterized by rolling hills, ancient granite boulder outcrops (known as "bolas"), and diverse woodlands. You'll cycle through expansive dehesa pastureland, Mediterranean scrub, and extensive oak forests, including unique Pyrenean oak woodlands. Some routes may feature unpaved or challenging segments, while others offer more gentle inclines.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Yes, many routes in the park are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363019169/dehesas-de-azuel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Azuel Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027121082/cardena-and-azuel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Azuel Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular ride.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The park is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can encounter distinctive granite formations, the deeply incised River Yeguas, and diverse Mediterranean forests. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic vistas, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo Bridge over the Yeguas River&lt;/a&gt;. The charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución de Cardeña&lt;/a&gt; also provides a cultural stop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the oak woodlands, and the opportunities to experience the region's rich biodiversity, including spotting various wildlife.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in the park?
A: While the park offers a range of routes, many are classified as moderate or difficult, involving rolling hills and varied terrain. For families, it's advisable to look for shorter, less strenuous sections or paths that are specifically designated as easy. Always check the route details for elevation gain and surface type to ensure it suits your family's abilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is a natural park with significant wildlife, including endangered species like the Iberian Lynx. While dogs are generally allowed on trails, they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety. Always check specific park regulations for any restricted areas or seasonal rules regarding pets.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making cycling challenging, while winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options in the park?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375134449/conquista-y-azuel-circular-desde-fuencaliente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conquista y Azuel — circular desde Fuencaliente&lt;/a&gt; cover over 50 km, offering extensive exploration of the park's diverse landscapes. These longer routes often feature significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: Parking is generally available near the main towns and access points to the park. For instance, routes often start or pass through areas like Cardeña or Azuel, where you can typically find places to park your vehicle. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see while cycling in the park?
A: Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is a crucial habitat for a wide range of wildlife. You might spot Red, Fallow, and Roe deer, Wild boar, and various raptors like Spanish Imperial Eagles or Black Vultures. It's also one of the last refuges for the endangered Iberian Lynx and home to a wolf population, though these are much harder to observe.
tips: In the center of the town of Cardeña there is the town hall, a square with a fountain, a store selling Iberian products from the area, and some bars with the gastronomic specialties of the region. The Iberian acorn-fed ham and the fried suckling pig are true gems that you must try.
Parallel to the road, a very beautiful path runs between oaks and pines.
It is a magnificent place to rest and, at night, see the stars in the heart of the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro natural park.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Venta del Charco y Azuel circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Venta del Charco y Azuel circular route is classified as difficult for touring cyclists. It demands a high level of fitness due to its length, elevation changes, and mixed terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of surfaces. While predominantly on paved roads, there are also unpaved segments that can be challenging to navigate. This combination contributes to its difficult rating.

faqs.question: Where does the Venta del Charco y Azuel circular route start?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Cardeña, specifically from the Plaza de la Constitución de Cardeña. This is also a good place to find parking.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the beautiful Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, known for its tranquil surroundings, soft hills, and rounded shapes. Expect picturesque landscapes with sections featuring a very beautiful path between oaks and pines, as well as immense seas of olive groves.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant highlight is the Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo, which is an excellent spot for resting and even stargazing at night. The route also includes the Path from Cardeña to Venta del Charco, offering scenic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle in the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the natural park is generally dog-friendly for hiking, this specific route is designed for touring bicycles. Cycling with dogs, especially on a difficult route with mixed terrain, can be challenging and is not typically recommended unless your dog is accustomed to running alongside a bike for long distances and you can ensure their safety and well-being.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Transandalus, Colada del Cerezo a la Venta del Charco, Cañizo Fariña, Colada del Cerezo a Azuel, and Vereda del Carrizo, among others.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness. Cyclists should be prepared for a significant distance (approximately 38.7 km) and an elevation gain of around 470 meters, combined with varied terrain.
